content.problemStatement:
Chef will give you an array AAA of length NNN which is a **permutation**. You can split the given array into some number of partitions, individually sort each subarray and join them back together without changing their relative order. After concatenating them, the result should a the sorted array. Find the **maximum**  number of partitions we can make.

**Note:** A permutation is an array consisting of NNN **distinct** integers from 111 to NNN in **arbitrary** order.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,..,AN A_1,A_2,..,A_NA1​,A2​,..,AN​ denoting the integers of the array.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line - the maximum number of partitions possible.
constraints: The array A is a permutation of 1…N
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the permutation. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, a permutation of {1,…,N}.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible number of partitions.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we cut after every position i where the maximum of the prefix equals i. The counts are 4, 3 and 1 respectively.
Prefix maxima: [2,2,3,5,5]. Cuts occur at i=2,3,5 → three partitions.

solution.approach:
Traverse the array from left to right while keeping the maximum value seen so far (curMax). At position i (1‑based) a cut can be placed if curMax == i, because the elements in the prefix are exactly the numbers 1…i (they are a permutation). Sorting each such segment independently will place the numbers in correct order, and cutting at every possible position yields the maximum number of partitions. Count how many positions satisfy curMax == i.

Algorithm per test case:
1. ans = 0, curMax = 0.
2. For i from 1 to N:
   a. read x = A[i].
   b. curMax = max(curMax, x).
   c. if curMax == i then ans++.
3. Output ans.

The algorithm is O(N) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array. Overall O(∑N).
